opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't catch my breath, my chest is tight, and my inhaler isn't doing anything. I felt like I was suffocating when I tried to lie down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Breathing got steadily worse through the evening and my puffer isn't helping like it normally does — I'm scared I can't get enough air, especially lying down.

symptom_narrative.onset: started around 8pm this evening with chest tightness and wheeze
symptom_narrative.duration: several hours and getting worse — by about 11pm I couldn't lie flat
symptom_narrative.character: tight chest and a whistling, wheezy sound when I breathe out; coughing fits when I lie down
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst my breathing has ever been — it feels like I'm suffocating and it's really frightening
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: much worse at night and worse when I lie down flat
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting upright and leaning forward helps a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down flat — it sets off the coughing and I feel like I can't breathe
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my nose is really blocked and stuffy like it often is; I feel very anxious and panicky. No fever, no chest pain, I'm not coughing up any coloured phlegm
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've got mild asthma and hay fever and usually a couple of puffs of my blue inhaler sorts it out — I've never had it this bad or had the inhaler just not work
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sleep or lie down, I've been sitting up in a chair to try to breathe

past_medical_history.how_managed: occasional puffs of a blue reliever inhaler when needed

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: hay fever / allergies in the nose that started when I was an adult
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: in adulthood, not as a child

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: keep getting polyps / growths in my nose and a constantly blocked nose

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; very anxious about the breathing tonight; bedroom is dusty with poor ventilation and there are pets in the home

objective_data_offered.value: I can still speak in full sentences but I'm breathing fast and it's a real effort
sitting upright and leaning forward on my hands to breathe more easily
I can hear myself wheezing when I breathe out
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own description of the sound, not an auscultated finding

objective_data_offered.value: the bedroom is dusty and stuffy with poor airflow, and there are pets around the house
